items.question: Which of the following is a vector space over R with the usual operations?
options: All real 2D vectors (x, y)
All points (x, y) in R^2 with x >= 0 and y >= 0
All non-zero vectors in R^2
All points (x, y) in R^2 with x + y = 1
items.explanation: R^2 with usual addition and scalar multiplication satisfies all vector space axioms. The other sets fail closure or do not contain the zero vector.

items.question: In R^3, which of the following subsets is a subspace?
options: All vectors (x, y, z) with z = 1
All vectors (x, y, z) with x + y + z = 0
All vectors (x, y, z) with x > 0
All vectors (x, y, z) with x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = 1
items.answer: All vectors (x, y, z) with x + y + z = 0
items.explanation: The set x + y + z = 0 contains 0 and is closed under addition and scalar multiplication, so it is a subspace. The other sets either exclude 0 or are not closed.

items.question: Which statement about a basis of a finite-dimensional vector space V is true?
options: Any spanning set is automatically a basis of V.
Any linearly independent set is automatically a basis of V.
All bases of V have the same number of vectors.
A basis of V must consist of orthonormal vectors.
items.answer: All bases of V have the same number of vectors.
items.explanation: The number of vectors in any basis is the dimension of V, so all bases have the same size. Spanning alone or independence alone is not sufficient, and orthonormality is a useful special case but not required.

items.question: In R^2, what is the span of the vectors (1, 0) and (2, 0)?
options: All of R^2
The x-axis
The y-axis
Only the zero vector
items.explanation: Both vectors lie on the x-axis, and any linear combination has form (a, 0), so the span is exactly the x-axis.

items.question: If a vector space V has a basis with 5 vectors, which statement is correct?
options: Every spanning set of V has exactly 5 vectors.
Every linearly independent set in V has at most 5 vectors.
There exists a basis of V with 4 vectors.
The dimension of V cannot be determined from this information.
items.answer: Every linearly independent set in V has at most 5 vectors.
items.explanation: If one basis has 5 vectors, the dimension of V is 5. No linearly independent set in V can have more than dimension(V) vectors.
